mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.0% as of June 2008 indicates easing cycle already underway
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following credit stress
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.3% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% as of June 2008 indicates labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 5.3%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 900 thousand units annualized, indicating weakening residential construction demand and reduced multiplier effects on employment and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 975 thousand units as of May 2008 shows housing weakness
regime_hint: housing market downturn with credit constraints
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 900, or Rank IC between HOUST and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month below 110.5, signaling contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and real economic output, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.9 as of May 2008 near recent highs
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when INDPRO < 110.5, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and housing starts move in opposite directions (retail sales decline while housing starts fall sharply), indicating a demand shock that simultaneously reduces consumer spending and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:Both retail sales and housing under pressure in mid-2008
regime_hint: synchronized demand collapse across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declines month-over-month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating accelerating labor market deterioration that signals imminent earnings downgrades and equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < -50 thousand AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market showing stress with UNRATE at 5.5% and employment pressures
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no statistically significant incremental explanatory power beyond univariate models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of monetary easing (FEDFUNDS < 2.5%), inflation measured by CPI remains above 2.5% year-over-year, creating real rate compression that incentivizes equity allocation and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.0% and CPIAUCSL at 215.1 indicate easing with inflation
regime_hint: monetary easing with above-target inflation creating negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the real rate compression signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth trajectory (GDPC1) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates demand shifting away from residential investment toward other sectors, but if HOUST falls below 850 units while GDP remains below 11,700 billion, it signals broad-based demand weakness producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,703.6 and HOUST at 975 show economy near threshold of broad weakness
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across residential and overall economic activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal fails to improve upon univariate HOUST model (Rank IC difference < 0.03).
